author: y4rr
content:
Spun out of #592. That issue landed greedy / temperature / top-k / top-p (nucleus) sampling + repetition penalty + streaming callback in \`ferrotorch-llama/src/generation.rs\`. Beam search and speculative decoding need separate passes.

## Beam search
Maintain \`num_beams\` candidate sequences ranked by cumulative log-probability. At each step:
1. For each live beam, run forward and get \`vocab_size\` continuation logits.
2. Add log-prob of each continuation to the beam's running score.
3. Take the top \`num_beams\` total scores across (beam × vocab) — this is the next round of beams.
4. Beams that hit EOS finalize and stop competing for slots.

Memory cost: \`num_beams\` × prompt cache. Compute cost: \`num_beams\` × per-token cost. Length normalization (\`length_penalty\`) is the standard trick for short-answer bias.

API sketch:
\`\`\`rust
pub fn beam_search(
    model: &LlamaForCausalLM<T>,
    prompt: &[u32],
    cfg: &BeamSearchConfig,  // num_beams, max_new_tokens, length_penalty, eos_ids
) -> FerrotorchResult<Vec<Vec<u32>>>;  // sorted best-first
\`\`\`

## Speculative decoding (Leviathan et al. 2023)
Use a small "draft" model to propose K tokens, then verify them with the big "target" model in a single forward pass. If the target accepts all K, you advance K tokens for one big-model forward. Acceptance criterion: target's prob ratio test.

Requires:
- Two model handles (draft + target), same tokenizer
- Per-step accept/reject probability check
- Rollback the cache on rejection

Easy 2-3× wallclock speedup on chat-style workloads where the draft model is much smaller.

## Notes
- Both belong in \`generation.rs\` alongside the existing sampling primitives.
- Speculative decoding's win shows up only on GPU paths where forward-pass overhead dominates; CPU users won't see much benefit.
